title: manulea juncea
intro: Manulea juncea, also known as the Cushion Bush or Swamp Manulea, is a perennial plant native to New Zealand, characterized by its dense, spreading habit and small white or purple flowers. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Manulea juncea include soothing sore throats, reducing fever, and aiding in the recovery from colds and flu. The plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used topically in the form of a salve or ointment to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
